content.full_text:
June 29, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento CA 95804-0807

Dear Sir/Madam:

Please provide me with written advice concerning the following issues:

Hypothetical 1.

A general purpose recipient committee loans money to a candidate controlled committee and the contribution is reported as a loan by both committees.

a. Can the candidate controlled committee repay the loan?

b. If so, how is the repayment reported?

c. Can the repayment only be made if the money goes into an account which is not used to make contributions to candidates?

d. If the general purpose committee (broad-based) contributed a total of $5000 to the candidate controlled committee ($2500 in one election and $2500 in another or $2500 in different fiscal years) can the candidate controlled committee pay it back all at once?

Hypothetical 2.

A general purposes recipient committee gives money to a candidate controlled committee and sends a letter along with the contribution which states that "if you win the election we expect you to pay back the amount given. If you lose, you do not have to repay the amount." Both the general purpose recipient committee and the candidate controlled committee report the donation as a contribution because at the time reporting is required, the election has not yet occurred. The candidate is thereafter elected and the general purpose recipient committee wants the donation repayed.

a. Can the candidate controlled committee repay the donation?

b. In order to repay, would the reports of each committee have to be amended to show the donation as a loan rather than a contribution?

c. If one committee reported it as a loan initially and the other committee reported it as a contribution, would the answer to the repayment question change?

d. (Same questions as in l. c. and l. d. above)

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

Allene C. Zanger

ACZ:mod
